concerns.text: Task T032 mandates using the Lincoln-Petersen Capture-Recapture estimator for precision/recall. This contradicts the data-flow defined in the Spec (FR-004, SC-001) and Plan, which require measuring against an expert-validated ground truth baseline derived from PR comments. Capture-Recapture implies two independent lists (Tool vs. Human) to estimate a hidden total, whereas the plan requires alignment (T027) to produce a single 'aligned_pairs' artifact (TP/FP/FN) for direct metric calculation. T032 consumes the wrong artifact type for the defined statistical method.

Task T036 implements Bonferroni correction. The Plan (Section: Computational Feasibility & Statistical Rigor) explicitly rejects Bonferroni as 'too conservative' and mandates a 'max-t permutation procedure' for FWER control. T036 creates a dependency on a statistical method that the plan has explicitly discarded, violating the producer-consumer flow of the statistical analysis pipeline.

Task T034 specifies a 'Fixed-effects regression' model. The Spec (FR-008) and Plan (Complexity Tracking) explicitly require 'Mixed-effects regression' (LMM) to handle project-level clustering and avoid overfitting with small N. T034 defines a model that does not match the required statistical artifact, breaking the dependency chain for the final research output.

Task T028 mandates implementing a ±5 line tolerance window for sensitivity analysis. The Plan (Phase 4) and Spec (FR-005) restrict this tolerance to a fallback mechanism when AST is unavailable, not as a primary sensitivity parameter. T028 creates a dependency on a parameter that is semantically misaligned with the plan's logic, potentially causing the sensitivity analysis (T025) to test the wrong variable.

Task T039 includes a post-hoc power analysis check. The Spec (Assumptions) and Plan treat the 30–40 repository sample size as a fixed constraint with mixed-effects models as the mitigation. A post-hoc power check on a fixed, small N is methodologically contradictory to the design and creates a dependency on a metric that the plan explicitly avoids to prevent misleading conclusions.
